subject: Physics
title: The Strong Nuclear Force
description: A quick, practical guide to The Strong Nuclear Force.

hook.text: Protons all carry positive charge. They should repel and fly apart—yet they stick together in the nucleus.

buildup.text: Electromagnetic repulsion pushes protons apart. Something stronger must be holding them in.

discovery.text: The strong nuclear force is 100 times more powerful than electromagnetism but only works at tiny distances.

twist.text: Beyond a femtometer, the strong force vanishes. It's like glue that only works when things touch.

climax.text: Without the strong force, nuclei would explode. No atoms beyond hydrogen could exist. No chemistry.

punchline.text: The strongest force in nature. It holds every atom together.

quiz.question: What keeps protons bound together in the nucleus despite their repulsion?
